actors.description: House servant with no personal name. Not a suspect.
instructions: Lock the dining wing from the outside once dinner begins. Do this discreetly so the guests do not notice.
Do not explain the bell code directly. Only respond to the appropriate number of rings:
• One bell: deliver drinks or tea, but do not open the wing.
• Two bells: this is the house signal to unlock the wing. Open the door after hearing two clear rings.
• Three bells: acknowledge, but do not act until summoned properly.
Use the scripted lines for each bell response from the speeches bank.
Avoid giving personal motives or becoming involved in accusations.

actors.description: Caretaker of the gardens. Not a suspect.
instructions: During Act 2, guide guests through the grounds in a methodical search.
Find the fake envelope near the terrace, rose bed, or gravel. Show that the wax stamp is wrong.
Observe facts but avoid solving the mystery. Use the scripted lines when the fake is exposed and at The Blind Judge statue.
Note the disturbed plaque and encourage the players to investigate the statue themselves.
Do not reveal the true hiding place of the real envelope until the players discover it.

actors.description: Legal representative reading the will. Dies before the codicil is read.
instructions: Give the legalistic speech explaining the purpose and procedure of the reading, using the provided script.
Pass the Black Envelope around the table and instruct the guests to verify its appearance without opening it.
After the blackout effect, lie on the dining room floor to simulate your murder.
Do not speak or move after Act 1 begins; you are not a suspect.

actors.description: Officer or pair of officers arriving in Act 3 to summarise evidence and lead accusations.
instructions: Enter at the beginning of Act 3 and call everyone back to the living room.
Summarise all discovered facts in a concise manner.
Facilitate the reading of the real codicil and comparison with the fake.
Encourage each guest to voice their motive and accusation.
Guide the group toward identifying the Director as the killer based on the evidence.
Ensure the pacing remains tight and prevents players from going off‑script for too long.
